一直负责并购和国际投资的Anthony Lin将临时接任。有趣的是，当布鲁克斯升职时，他也负责并购业务。
林书豪是否会继续担任这一角色还有待观察，但当我在2015年与布鲁克斯交谈时，当时他即将接替索德哈尼，他听起来肯定已经准备好接受手头的任务。他当时说：“要保持这样的记录，我还有很长的路要走。”我认为这是一个扩大组织重点的巨大机会，我们可以在这里为投资组合的公司提供战略价值。“。
在同一次采访中，布鲁克斯描述了他的投资哲学，说他更喜欢领导，而不是作为二级投资者。他说：“我倾向于认为，与被动投资者相比，首席投资者能够更多地影响初创公司的商业论点、进入市场的路线、方向和技术。”他补充说，它还倾向于获得能够提供额外影响力的董事会席位。
他将他的公司与传统的风险投资公司进行了比较，他说，就投资记录而言，他们和传统的风险投资公司一样好，甚至更好，作为战略投资者，他们也带来了其他一些优势。“一些传统的风投专注于打造公司价值。我们可以提供战略指导，并与其他风险投资公司相比，对一些公司的建设表示赞赏。“。
在该公司的整个生命周期中，它已经向1500多家公司投资了129亿美元，其中692家公司通过IPO或收购退出。就在今年，在布鲁克斯的领导下，该公司迄今投资了2.25亿美元，其中包括11笔新投资和26笔对投资组合中已有公司的投资。
note_en:
“ Wendell Brooks   has resigned from  Intel   to pursue other opportunities. We thank Wendell for all his contributions and wish him the best for the future,” a company spokesperson told TechCrunch in a rather bland send off.
 Anthony Lin, who has been leading mergers and acquisitions and international investing, will take over on an interim basis. Interestingly, when Brooks was promoted, he too was in charge of mergers and acquisitions.
  Whether Lin keeps that role remains to be seen, but when I spoke to Brooks in 2015 as he was about to take over from Sodhani, he certainly sounded ready for the task at hand. “I have huge shoes to fill in maintaining that track record,” he said at the time. “I view it as a huge opportunity to grow the focus of organization where we can provide strategic value to portfolio companies.”
 In that same interview, Brooks described his investment philosophy, saying he preferred to lead, rather than come on as a secondary investor. “I tend to think the lead investor is able to influence the business thesis, the route to market, the direction, the technology of a startup more than a passive investor,” he said. He added that it also tends to get board seats that can provide additional influence.
 Comparing his firm to traditional VC firms, he said they were as good or better in terms of the investing record, and as a strategic investor brought some other advantages as well. “Some of the traditional VCs are focused on a company building value. We can provide strategic guidance and compliment some of the company building over other VCs,” he said.
 Over the life of the firm, it has invested $12.9 billion in over 1,500 companies with 692 of those exiting via IPO or acquisition. Just this year, under Brooks’ leadership, the company invested $225 million so far, including 11 new investments and 26 investments in companies already in the portfolio.

这项关键的十年一次的统计决定了从国会代表到佩尔拨款再到学校午餐项目资金的方方面面-截至本周，多达6000万户家庭仍下落不明。如果不统计，当分配关键的联邦资源时，这些个人和他们的社区将是看不见的。
为了迎接这一挑战，进步志愿者和运动协调平台Mobiled正在启动一个中央资源中心，以在六周的最后阶段增强人口普查志愿者的能力。这家公民科技初创公司注意到，少数从事人口普查工作的非营利性组织已经在该平台上开展活动，新网站GetOutTheCount.com将扩大这些努力，并将它们收集在一个地方。
在接受TechCrunch采访时，Mobility联合创始人兼首席执行官阿尔弗雷德·约翰逊合理地将这项任务描述为“艰巨的任务”。
组织正在努力联系社区，帮助他们了解在人口普查中会被问到什么，人口普查中不会问到什么，并确保…。这些社区意识到他们在这里的权利是什么，知道最后期限是什么，而且是可以计数的。“约翰逊说。“因为我们知道，被计算在内是我们民主的一项基本内容。”
今年人口普查年最大的挑战之一是专注于接触到历史上被低估的黑人、拉丁裔和土著社区-如果2020年人口普查是为了捕捉美国人口变化，并相应地分配资源和代表性，这是一个关键目标。
动员运动于2017年初在特朗普后左翼激进主义浪潮中发起，并迅速在进步事业和候选人中无处不在。在2020年的民主党初选中，拜登、伯尼和中间的每个人都依靠这个平台来召集竞选志愿者和引导支持者。今年1月，这家公民科技初创公司筹集了375万美元的首轮融资，由进步科技孵化器Higher Ground Labs牵头。LinkedIn的联合创始人里德·霍夫曼(Reid Hoffman)是一位著名的民主党捐赠者，克里斯·萨卡(Chris Sacca)的Lowercase Capital也为这轮融资做出了贡献。
这个数字平台的目标是既成为民主党和进步阵营的统一资源，又能做Facebook等社交网络上的活动页面做不到的事情。对于Mobiled来说，这意味着将不同平台上可能仍有抱负的在线活动转化为行动。它通过向志愿者发送提醒，促使他们邀请朋友，即使在他们采取行动让他们参与类似的活动后也保持联系，从而实现了这一点。
已经在动员上协调人口普查活动的组织包括纽约市人口普查局、CensusCounts和公平计数，这是一个由公平斗争创始人、佐治亚州州长前民主党提名人斯泰西·艾布拉姆斯创立的组织。公平计数的使命是接触到它所说的佐治亚州“难以统计”的社区，包括该州历史上被低估的黑人男性人口，为该州赢得反映其现实的资源和代表性。
GetOutTheCount.com允许任何人输入他们的邮政编码，以查看这些组织和其他组织之间协调的当地人口普查动员工作。顺理成章的是，如果你愿意打电话给银行去联系那些还没有被计入某一群体的人，你可能会愿意为目标重叠的不同群体这样做。
对于Mobility来说，关键的最终人口普查推动在全数字化的一年里对该平台的力量来说是一个熔炉。随着新冠肺炎在美国扎根，约翰逊看到虚拟活动在动员中飙升。在大流行之前，大约四分之一的活动是虚拟的-现在都是虚拟的。
约翰逊承认，反对2020年人口普查准确统计的“逆风”是非常真实的，无论是在政治上还是在后勤上，特别是在特朗普政府已经削减了最后期限的情况下。但他希望，在国家危机期间，移动能够帮助组织利用平台的网络效应和可扩展性的力量，而不是敲门。
尽管发生了危机，或许正因为如此，Mobilize在4月至7月期间看到志愿者注册人数大幅上升，预计一旦人数增加，8月份的注册人数将会更多。
约翰逊说：“出于非常真实的原因，2020年对很多人来说是非常艰难的一年。”“我认为，这实际上是在激发更多的公民参与，因为人们希望看到情况发生变化，并在这个事关生存的危机时刻帮助他们的朋友、邻居(和)社区，无论你在评估什么轴心。”
note_en:
The critical once-a-decade count determines everything from Congressional representation to Pell grants to funding for school lunch programs — and as of this week, as many as  60 million households remain unaccounted for. If left untallied, those individuals and their communities will be invisible when the time comes to allocate vital federal resources.
 To rise to that challenge, the progressive volunteer and campaign coordination platform  Mobilize is launching a central resource hub to empower census volunteers during the six week final stretch. The civic tech startup noticed that a handful of nonprofits doing census work were already bringing campaigns onto the platform, and the new site,  GetOutTheCount.com, will amplify those efforts and collect them in one place.
   Speaking with TechCrunch, Mobilize Co-Founder and CEO Alfred Johnson describes the task, reasonably, as “Herculean.”
 “Organizations are trying to reach communities and help them understand what they’re going to be asked in the census, what they’ll not be asked by the census and make sure… that those communities are aware of what their rights are here, are aware of what the deadlines are, and can be counted,” Johnson said.  “Because we know that being counted is such a fundamental piece of being included in our democracy.”
 One of the biggest challenges this census year is the focus on reaching  historically undercounted Black, Latinx  and indigenous communities — a key goal if the 2020 census is to capture U.S. demographic shifts and allocate resources and representation accordingly.
 Mobilize launched in early 2017 amidst the post-Trump surge of activism on the left and quickly became ubiquitous among progressive causes and candidates. In the 2020 Democratic primary contest, Biden, Bernie and everybody in between relied on the platform to marshal campaign volunteers and steer supporters. This January, the civic tech  startup raised a $3.75 million Series A round led by progressive tech incubator  Higher Ground Labs. LinkedIn co-founder Reid Hoffman, a prominent Democratic donor, and Chris Sacca’s Lowercase Capital also contributed to the round.
  The digital platform aims to both be a unifying resource to Democratic and progressive campaigns and to do what the events page on social networks like Facebook can’t. For Mobilize, that means translating what on a different platform might remain aspirational online activity into action. It accomplishes that by sending volunteers reminders, prompting them to invite friends and staying connected even after they take action to keep them engaged in similar campaigns.
 Groups already coordinating their census campaigns on Mobilize include the  NYC Census Bureau,   CensusCounts , and  Fair Count, an organization founded by  Fair Fight founder and former Democratic nominee for Georgia governor Stacey Abrams. Fair Count’s mission is to reach what it calls “hard to count” communities in Georgia, including the state’s historically undercounted Black male population, to win the state the resources and representation that reflect its reality.
 GetOutTheCount.com lets anyone type in their zip code to see local census mobilization efforts coordinated across those organizations and others. It stands to reason that if you’re willing to phone bank to reach people who’ve yet to be counted for one group, you’d probably be willing to do it for a different one with overlapping goals.
 For Mobilize, the crucial final census push is something of a crucible for the platform’s power in a year that’s gone all-digital. Johnson has seen virtual events skyrocket on Mobilize as COVID-19 took root across the U.S. Prior to the pandemic, about a quarter of events were virtual — now they all are.
 Johnson acknowledges that the “headwinds” against an accurate census count in 2020 are very real, both politically and logistically, and particularly now that the Trump administration has trimmed the deadline. But he hopes that Mobilize is able to help organizations leverage the power of the platform’s network effect and its scalability during a national crisis that has a nation cooped up indoors rather than knocking on them.
 In spite of the crisis, or perhaps because of it, Mobilize has seen a major uptick in volunteer signups between the months of April and July and expects August to be even bigger once the numbers are in.
 “2020 is a very hard year for a lot of people for very real reasons,” Johnson said.  “I think that is actually motivating even more civic engagement by virtue of the fact that people are wanting to see circumstances change and help their friends, neighbors [and] communities in this moment of existential crisis, on whatever axis you’re evaluating it.”

文化、生产力和速度对早期初创公司的生存绝对至关重要，但如何在一个仅限于远程的世界中建立增长和动力呢？投资者如何看待这个新环境以及我们不断变化的工作模式对我们意味着的机遇？
这些都是至关重要的问题，这就是为什么我们要主办一个风险投资者超级明星小组，在TechCrunch Disrupt2020的Extra Crunch舞台上更多地谈论他们。
首先，我们有指数风险投资公司(Index Ventures)的合伙人萨拉·坎农(Sarah Cannon)，她最近在硅谷最出名的可能是她雄心勃勃地押注于生产力工具概念(Productivity Tool Options)，后者对这家相对新兴的初创公司的估值高达20亿美元。Canon还支持即时通讯应用Quill和Pitch，后者提供围绕演示文档的协作。未来的工作一直是她的谋生之本，我们很高兴听到她对下一步生产力的看法，以及创业公司未来将如何发展。
接下来，我们请到了Greylock的普通合伙人莎拉·郭(Sarah Guo)。郭还一直在投资未来的工作和B2B工具，包括俱乐部(不是你想的俱乐部)，这有助于开发团队更有效地协作。此外，她还支持家庭福利平台Cleo和一整套网络安全公司-随着工作场所的传统周边已被分散在世界各地的员工笔记本电脑取代，这一领域已变得至关重要。
第三位也是最后一位嘉宾是GV的普通合伙人戴夫·穆尼奇洛(Dave Munichiello)。他投资了一个名为SLACK的社交工具(我拒绝称它为生产力工具，但这可能是一个人的观点)，还有远程初创公司GitLab，它的估值已超过30亿美元，以及金融科技基础设施公司Played，后者去年以53亿美元的价格卖给了维萨，这是2019年金融科技最大的退出之一。
从如何打造产品到如何组建团队，再到在我们这个疯狂的流行病世界里，投资者在初创企业中寻找的是什么，这个小组已经让你畅所欲言。另外，由于我们在Disrupt2020上处于额外的嘎吱作响阶段，我们将在整个讨论过程中回答观众的问题。所以，快来加入我们的对话吧，让我们一起来了解2020对这个十年的创业界意味着什么。
今天就拿到通行证，扰乱2020年。它是100%虚拟的，这意味着我们100%想在那里见到你！
note_en:
Culture, productivity, and speed are absolutely vital to the survival of early-stage startups, but how do you build growth and momentum in a remote-only world? And how are investors approaching this new environment and the opportunities that our changing patterns of work mean for us?
 These are critical questions, which is why we are hosting a panel of VC investor superstars to talk more about them on the Extra Crunch stage at  TechCrunch Disrupt 2020.
  First, we have Sarah Cannon, partner at Index Ventures who is perhaps best known in the Valley these days for  her ambitious bet behind  productivity tool Notion, which valued the relatively nascent startup at a cool $2 billion. Cannon has also backed messaging app Quill as well as Pitch, which offers collaboration around presentation documents. Future of work has been her bread and butter, and we’re excited to hear what she thinks is next in productivity and how startups will grow going forward.
 Next, we have Sarah Guo, who is a general partner at Greylock. Guo also has been investing in the future of work and B2B tools including Clubhouse (not the Clubhouse you are thinking about)  which helps dev teams collaborate more effectively. In addition, she has backed family benefits platform Cleo and a panoply of cybersecurity companies — an area that has become acutely important as the classic perimeter of the workplace office has been replaced with employee laptops scattered across locations worldwide.
 Third and finally on this panel, we have Dave Munichiello, who is a general partner at GV. He’s backed a little social tool called Slack (I refuse to call it a productivity tool but that might be one person’s opinion), as well as that remote-first startup Gitlab, which has  received upwards of a $3 billion valuation, and fintech infra company Plaid,  which was sold to Visa last year for $5.3 billion in one of the biggest fintech exits of 2019.
 From how to build products to how to build teams to what investors are looking for in startups in our crazy pandemic world, this panel has got you covered. Plus, since we are on the Extra Crunch stage at  Disrupt 2020, we will be taking audience questions throughout the discussion. So come join the conversation as we figure out what 2020 means for the startup world this decade.
 Get your pass today to  Disrupt 2020. It’s 100% virtual which means we 100% want to see you there!

除了硬件发布，昨天三星发布会上更大的收获之一是它与微软的关系越来越密切。这两家公司在从手机游戏捆绑包到优化他们的应用程序和整合他们的软件的方方面面都在合作。他们甚至宣布，你很快就可以在Windows10 PC上直接使用和控制三星手机上的多个应用程序，并将三星平板电脑用作辅助显示器。
这并不是一种新的合作关系，微软首席执行官萨蒂亚·纳德拉(Satya Nadella)甚至在去年的三星Note 10发布会上亲自亮相。但随着业界冲向iOS和Android之间的下一场生态系统大战，这一点比以往任何时候都更加重要。这不再仅仅是手机本身的问题，而是这些手机如何与你生活中的其他电脑互动的问题，无论它们是笔记本电脑、台式机，甚至是游戏机。而三星与微软不断加深的合作伙伴关系对其做法至关重要。
三星和微软昨天宣布了许多共同开发的项目，涵盖了从游戏到办公应用和生产力软件的一切。
在游戏方面，焦点是微软的XCloud游戏流媒体服务。这些功能建立在现有的XCloud合作伙伴关系基础上，包括三星Galaxy Store上的Xbox Game Pass应用程序的一个特殊版本，该应用程序将提供Google Play Store版本中没有的功能。即将推出的Note 20还有一个游戏捆绑包，其中包括三个月的Xbox Game Pass旗舰版，以及一个专为玩流媒体游戏而设计的第三方控制器。
他们的生产力和办公软件也越来越紧密地融合在一起。来自Samsung Notes应用的笔记将与Outlook和OneNote同步，并且三星提醒将跨多个Microsoft服务同步，包括Microsoft To Do、Teams和Outlook。三星还表示，它正在与微软合作，为Galaxy Z Fold 2的显示优化PowerPoint、Word和Excel等应用程序，甚至还有一些小的合作，比如能够将Galaxy Tab S7用作Windows 10机器的外部显示器。
但最重要的功能，也是三星和微软合作带来的好处最清晰的例子，是新的Your Phone功能。昨天，微软宣布Windows10测试者将能够与Windows程序并行运行Android应用程序，目前，它只适用于三星手机。三星在活动期间表示，该功能将在今年晚些时候扩展，让你同时运行多个应用程序。
我毫不怀疑这一功能已经酝酿了一段时间，但很难不把它与苹果在WWDC 2020上的演示进行比较，在演示中，由于即将过渡到基于ARM的处理器，iOS和iPadOS应用程序在MacOS上本地运行。苹果和三星似乎采取了两种不同的方式--苹果的机器在本地运行移动应用程序，而三星的机器似乎是从联网的手机上流媒体播放--但最终结果是相似的：在你的电脑上使用移动应用程序。
而制造Android的谷歌似乎几乎没有出现在对话中。字面意思：三星直到主题演讲的最后三分之一才被提及。谷歌的生态系统解决方案是允许Android应用在Chrome OS上运行，以及一些次要的Android/Chrome OS集成，比如Tethering。但这一功能历来存在缺陷，Chrome OS总体上没有设法超越其历史上的利基市场。
不过，看看这三种产品，你就会清楚地看到一种趋势：手机、平板电脑和笔记本电脑之间的界限正变得比以往任何时候都更加模糊。我的同事迪特尔·博恩一直在问“什么是电脑？”多年来，这是一个越来越难回答的问题，因为每一个在传统平板电脑上添加键盘或触摸板的配件，或者在笔记本电脑上添加触摸屏的配件，都会变得越来越难以回答。
与苹果相比，这一趋势可能会让三星处于劣势。苹果已经对其移动和计算生态系统进行了足够的控制，可以提供连续性等跨外形功能，但随着苹果转向在Mac上使用基于ARM的处理器，它将获得一个统一的应用生态系统，最终可能会从手机一直延伸到Mac Pro等专业台式电脑。
苹果对其设备的方方面面都有控制权，这意味着它可以单独在手机、平板电脑和电脑之间划清界限。苹果可以决定让iPad作为Mac的辅助显示屏，而不必与三星和微软达成任何协议，以获得安卓系统驱动的Tab S7和Windows 10的相同功能。苹果可以单独为iPadOS添加重要的新功能，使其可以与外部鼠标和触摸板配合使用；它自己要负责决定MacBooks不应该有触摸屏；它自己把MacOS和iPadOS推到了相互冲突的道路上。
这种实力差距意味着，如果三星想让自己的产品像苹果一样无缝协作，它现在比以往任何时候都更需要微软这样的合作伙伴。作为Android的主要开发者，谷歌是另一个非常重要的合作伙伴，但Android的开源核心给了三星更大的自由，可以通过其One UI软件在其基础上构建自己的功能。
与苹果的庞然大物相比，三星的做法有其优势。三星没有试图在一个有围墙的花园里做所有的事情，而是能够顺从微软最大的优势。以XCloud为例。就在Xbox的菲尔·斯宾塞在三星展示Xbox正在与三星合作提供“最佳游戏体验”的同一天，三星结束了在iOS设备上的XCloud游戏流媒体测试。苹果的App Store政策被认为是问题所在。
拆分意味着三星手机用户和安卓社区的其他用户将可以访问XCloud的游戏库，而苹果用户只能玩在设备上本地运行的游戏(谷歌的Stadia游戏流媒体服务也不能在iOS上使用)。这仍然包括为iPhone和iPad开发的大量游戏，这些游戏很快就会在基于ARM的Mac上原生运行，还包括许多来自苹果自己的订阅服务的优秀游戏，但不包括Forza和Gears of War系列中的大型AAA Xbox游戏。
原生游戏和流媒体游戏不是一回事，但三星用户两者都有，而苹果用户只有一个。
我去年写过，三星在与苹果和谷歌的斗争中不会单打独斗，昨天的Note 20发布会再清楚不过了。智能手机、笔记本电脑和平板电脑的未来正在碰撞，三星无法控制自己开辟一条道路。然而，由于它与微软的合作伙伴数量不断增加，它有一个合作伙伴可以帮助它实现这一目标。
note_en:
Outside of its hardware announcements, one of the bigger takeaways from yesterday’s Samsung event was its ever-closer relationship with Microsoft. The two companies are partnering together on everything from  mobile gaming bundles, to  optimizing their apps and integrating their software. They even announced that you’ll soon be able to use and  control multiple apps from your Samsung phone directly on your Windows 10 PC, as well as use your Samsung tablet as a secondary display.
 It’s not a new partnership, and  Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella even personally appeared at Samsung’s Note 10 event last year. But it’s more important than ever before, as the industry hurtles toward the next big ecosystem battle between iOS and Android. It’s no longer just about the phones themselves but about how these phones interact with the  other computers in your life, whether they’re laptops, desktops, or even game consoles. And Samsung’s deepening partnership with Microsoft is essential to its approach.
   Samsung and Microsoft announced a lot of codeveloped projects yesterday, encompassing everything from gaming, to office apps and productivity software.
  On the gaming side, the focus was Microsoft’s xCloud game streaming service. The features, which  build on an existing xCloud partnership, include a  special version of the Xbox Game Pass app on Samsung’s Galaxy Store that will come with features not available in the Google Play Store version. There’s also a gaming bundle for the Note 20 coming that will include three months of Xbox Game Pass Ultimate alongside a third-party controller designed to play streamed games.
 Their  productivity and office software is also getting more deeply integrated. Notes from the Samsung Notes app will sync with Outlook and OneNote, and Samsung Reminders will sync across multiple Microsoft services including Microsoft To Do, Teams, and Outlook. Samsung also said that it was  working with Microsoft to optimize apps like PowerPoint, Word, and Excel for the display of the Galaxy Z Fold 2, and there were even minor tie-ups like being able to  use the Galaxy Tab S7 as an external display for Windows 10 machines.
   But the most important feature, and the one that gives the clearest example of the benefits of Samsung and Microsoft’s partnership, was the new Your Phone functionality. Yesterday, Microsoft announced that Windows 10 testers would be able to  run Android apps side by side with Windows programs and, at the moment, it works  exclusively with Samsung phones.  Samsung said during its event that the functionality would expand to let you run  multiple apps side by side later this year.
 I don’t doubt that this feature has been in the works for some time, but it’s hard not to compare it to  Apple’s demo from WWDC 2020 where it showed  iOS and iPadOS apps running natively on macOS thanks to its  upcoming transition to ARM-based processors. Apple and Samsung appear to be taking two different approaches here — Apple’s machines are running mobile apps natively while Samsung’s appear to be streaming from a connected phone — but the end result is similar: using mobile apps on your computer.
  And Google — which  makes Android — hardly seems to be in the conversation. Literally: the company didn’t get a mention  until the final third of Samsung’s keynote. Google’s ecosystem solution is to allow Android apps to run on top of Chrome OS as well as some minor Android / Chrome OS integrations like tethering. But the feature has been historically buggy and Chrome OS in general hasn’t managed to expand beyond its historical niches.
 Look at all three, though, and you can clearly see a trend: the boundaries between what is a phone, a tablet, and a laptop are becoming blurrier than ever. My colleague Dieter Bohn has been asking “ What’s a computer?” for years now, and it’s a question that’s getting increasingly difficult to answer with every accessory  that adds a keyboard or trackpad to a traditional tablet, or a touchscreen to a laptop.
 It’s a trend that may put Samsung at a disadvantage compared to Apple. Apple already controls enough about its mobile and computing ecosystems that it can offer cross form-factor features like  Continuity, but with its switch to using ARM-based processors on the Mac, it will gain a unified app ecosystem that could eventually stretch from phones all the way up to professional desktop computers like the Mac Pro.
 Apple’s control over every aspect of its devices means it alone can draw the boundaries between its phones, tablets, and computers. Apple can decide to  make the iPad work as a secondary display for the Mac, without having to make whatever agreement Samsung and Microsoft did to get the same functionality for the Android-powered Tab S7 and Windows 10. Apple alone can add significant new features to iPadOS to make it work with external mice and trackpads; it alone is responsible for deciding that MacBooks shouldn’t have touchscreens; and it alone has put macOS and iPadOS  on a collision course with one another.
   This power disparity means Samsung needs partners like Microsoft now more than ever if it wants its products to work together as seamlessly as Apple’s do. As the primary developer of Android, Google is another immensely important partner, but Android’s open-source core gives Samsung more latitude to build its own features on top of it via its One UI software.
 Against the monolith of Apple, Samsung’s approach has its advantages. Rather than trying to do everything inside one walled garden, Samsung is able to defer to Microsoft’s biggest strengths. Take xCloud. The same day Xbox’s Phil Spencer appeared during Samsung’s demonstration that Xbox was working with Samsung to offer the “best possible gaming experience,” it  ended its xCloud game streaming test on iOS devices. Apple’s App Store policies are thought to be the problem.
  The split means that Samsung phone owners, along with the rest of the Android community, will get access to xCloud’s library of games, while Apple users are limited to playing games that run natively across their devices (Google’s Stadia game streaming service is also not available on iOS). That still includes plenty of games made for the iPhone and iPad which will soon run natively on ARM-based Macs, and it also includes  many great games from  Apple’s own subscription service, but it won’t include big AAA Xbox titles like those from the  Forza and  Gears of War series.
 Native games are not the same thing as streamed games, but Samsung users get both, while Apple’s only get one.
 I wrote last year that  Samsung isn’t going it alone in its fight against Apple and Google, and yesterday’s Note 20 event couldn’t have made that clearer. The futures of smartphones, laptops, and tablets are colliding, and Samsung doesn’t have the control to be able to forge a path on its own. Thanks to its ever increasing number of partnerships with Microsoft, however, it has a partner that can help it get there.
title: 随着来自苹果统一生态系统的竞争压力越来越大，微软和三星现在比以往任何时候都更需要对方
title_en: As the competitive pressure from the Apple's unified ecosystem grows, Microsoft and Samsung need each other now more than ever

欢迎收看火箭报道的3.11版！本周许多最有趣的消息来自小发射领域，电子公司宣布迅速重返飞行，同时提高其电子助推器的容量。我们也惊讶地看到ABL空间系统公司如此有力的筹款努力。
一如既往，我们欢迎读者提交，如果您不想错过任何一期，请使用下面的框进行订阅(表单将不会显示在启用AMP的网站版本上)。每一份报告都将包括关于小型、中型和重型火箭的信息，以及日历上未来三次发射的快速展望。
阿斯特拉试图发射第二枚轨道火箭。从该公司位于阿拉斯加州科迪亚克岛的发射场发射火箭3.1的发射窗口于周日晚间开放。火箭和地面系统的技术问题，以及天气问题，使得周日至周三的发射无法进行。
滑向窗户的尽头。该公司现在的目标是在8月6日(星期四)飞行，窗口在太平洋标准时间晚上7点(世界协调时周五02：00)开放。这次飞行的目标是通过第一阶段的发射，它不会携带任何商业有效载荷。该公司首席执行官克里斯·坎普上周告诉记者，点亮第二级并进入轨道将是一件轻而易举的事情。我们祝愿阿斯特拉一切顺利。(Ken the Bin提交)。
维珍银河将商业航班推迟到2021年。据SpaceNews报道，维珍银河已经将其SpaceShipTwo亚轨道飞行器的商业飞行开始时间推迟到不早于2021年第一季度，同时宣布计划出售额外的股票以筹集资金。该公司在周一发布的第二财季财务业绩中表示，预计将在新墨西哥州的Spaceport America再进行两次SpaceShipTwo测试飞行，这两次飞行都将是动力飞行。
然后，骑士号……假设这次载人飞行一切顺利，我们将计划让理查德·布兰森爵士搭乘第三架动力飞机从新墨西哥州起飞，该公司首席太空官乔治·T·怀特赛兹(George T.Whiteside)说。这将标志着商业服务的开始。在这一点上，我们是在维珍银河的商业太空旅游计划上的当我们看到它的时候会相信它的阵营中的一员。在这一点上，我们是在维珍银河的商业太空旅游计划上的当我们看到它的时候会相信它的阵营。(JohnCarter17提交)。
跟上埃里克·伯杰的空间报道最简单的方法是注册他的时事通讯，我们会在你的收件箱里收集他的故事。
火箭实验室宣布本月重返飞行。火箭实验室(Rocket Lab)表示，在2020年7月4日发现异常电气连接是飞行中故障的原因后，它已获得美国联邦航空管理局(Federal Aviation Administration)的批准，本月可以恢复发射，这是一个相当快的扭亏为盈。调查能够自信地将问题缩小到单个异常电气连接。
火箭实验室负责人彼得·贝克(Peter Beck)表示，针对8月份的返航任务，这个问题发生在令人难以置信的具体和独特的情况下，导致连接失败，而我们用标准测试无法检测到。我们的团队现在已经在测试中可靠地复制了这个问题，并确定通过额外的测试和程序可以缓解这个问题。另外，火箭实验室还宣布将电子产品的有效载荷能力从225公斤增加到300公斤，进入近地轨道，这一点令人印象深刻。(由Trimeta，Unrulycow，Ken the Bin和platykurtic提交)。
ABL获得了新的资金。ABL Space Systems是一家成立三年的初创公司，为小卫星开发运载火箭。该公司本周宣布，它已经收到了两份价值4450万美元的美国空军合同，并获得了4900万美元的新私人资金。ABL表示，它正计划在2021年进行RS1飞行器的首次轨道发射。
关于大流行期间的筹款……我们确实在新冠肺炎大流行期间结束了这一轮。创始人兼首席财务官丹·皮埃蒙特在接受“太空新闻”采访时表示：毫无疑问，今年很多人的生活都面临着严峻的挑战，但我认为，关于小型发射资金被完全冻结、需要行业层面的救助的言论有点夸张了。这是一笔不小的钱，而且该公司的技术似乎正在走向成熟。我们会急切地想看看ABL的去向。(JohnCarter17和Ken the Bin提交)。
维珍轨道将在第二次发射时搭载NASA的有效载荷。在一篇长篇博客文章中，维珍轨道公司表示，它已经确定了第一次发射尝试失败的根本原因：由于部件故障，将低温液氧输送到我们第一级燃烧室的高压管道破裂。没有氧化剂的供应，发动机很快就停止提供推力，结束了我们的动力飞行，并最终结束了测试本身。
正在更新第二次发射演示任务...。该公司表示，飞行硬件正在进行最后的集成，不久将运往加利福尼亚州的莫哈韦进行测试。值得注意的是，对于第二次任务，美国国家航空航天局(NASA)已经同意为其立方体卫星发射计划发射11颗立方体卫星。这表明美国宇航局的部分风险承受能力令人钦佩，维珍部分也有一些信心。该公司计划在今年年底进行这次飞行。
阿丽亚娜五号火箭现在的目标是八月十四号，七月三十一号，在倒计时不到三分钟的时候，一枚载有三颗卫星的阿丽亚娜五号火箭的发射被叫停。原因呢？涉及主级液氢储罐内传感器的意外行为的技术问题。
从大流行中归来..。随后，火箭被转移回法属圭亚那库鲁发射场的总装大楼。本周，阿丽亚娜空间公司宣布了这次任务的新发射日期，8月14日&34；，并有可能对提前一天执行的任务过程进行优化。这是一项重要的任务，也是自今年早些时候由于新冠肺炎关闭航天港以来第一次从口柔起飞的任务。(Ken the Bin提交)。
延迟之后，星链任务返回发射台。据SpaceFirst Now报道，由于未披露的原因，SpaceX推迟了六周，周四在佛罗里达州肯尼迪航天中心的发射台上举起了一架猎鹰9号垂直发射台。周五早些时候，该公司将再次尝试发射公司的下一批Starlink互联网中继站和两颗商业BlackSky地球成像微型卫星。
深夜发射。发射定于世界协调时周五下午5点12分。这将是SpaceX首次发射搭载全套配备了新太阳罩或护目镜的Starlink卫星的发射，目的是降低地面望远镜对航天器的可见度，消除天文学家对数千颗Starlink卫星可能干扰科学观测的担忧。
韩国考虑退出自己的发射计划。韩国国防工业分析人士说，韩国将很难在发射行业与其他国家竞争，这些国家可以利用已经建立的航天工业基础设施，以更低的价格提供更先进的技术和服务。韩国国防与安全论坛(Korea Defense And Security Forum)高级研究员申钟宇(Shin Jong-woo)在接受“韩国时报”(Korea Times)采访时表示，即使韩国自行研发火箭，它们在技术方面也无法超越SpaceX等航空航天巨头。
是成本的十倍..。韩国太空研究所卡里(Kari)已经开发了几枚小卫星火箭，但这些火箭无法与SpaceX的猎鹰9号(Falcon 9)竞争，猎鹰9号最近发射了韩国第一颗军事通信卫星ANASIS-II。韩国航空工业公司(Kari)前总裁金升洙(Kim Seung-jo)表示，韩国政府开发这两枚火箭的成本是SpaceX的10倍。但韩国政府在火箭项目上的成果远远落后于SpaceX生产的猎鹰9号火箭(由JohnCarter17提交)。
SES选择SpaceX和ULA进行商业发射。在不同的新闻稿中，SES宣布与美国两家领先的发射公司达成协议。SpaceX将通过两次猎鹰9号发射为其最多三颗C波段卫星提供发射能力，一颗联合发射联盟Atlas V将携带这两颗堆叠的卫星。只有美国火箭被考虑用于商业任务。
这次没有火神..。C波段卫星的发射将促进5G网络在美国各地的分布。这一声明值得注意的原因有两个：首先，ULA赢得纯商业合同的情况很少见。其次，加州大学洛杉矶分校的任务将使用阿特拉斯V号，而不是火神火箭。这可能表明，代表SES&#39；对2022年瓦肯星的准备情况缺乏信心。(JohnCarter17和Ken the Bin提交)。
蜂拥着搭乘猎鹰9号搭便车下水。集群技术公司正在与德国Exolaunch公司合作，将24颗SpaceBee卫星送入轨道，执行SpaceX猎鹰9号小卫星拼车任务，计划于12月发射。据SpaceNews报道，Exolaunch是一家拼车发射和卫星部署公司，将在太阳同步轨道上处理SpaceBee卫星的发射、集成和部署。
目标是十二月..。今年早些时候，Exolaunch宣布与SpaceX达成协议，将在12月的拼车飞行中将多颗小卫星送入轨道。对于12月的发射，Exolaunch计划将微型卫星和立方体卫星集成到猎鹰9号进化可消费运载火箭二次有效载荷适配器端口上，并利用该公司的专有部署系统EXOpod将卫星送入轨道。(Ken the Bin和JohnCarter17提交)。
阿特拉斯5号向火星的发射肯定是华丽的。上周四，阿特拉斯V号火箭发射了美国宇航局最新的火星车“毅力”前往这颗红色星球。在这次任务中，NASA选择了一枚配有四个固体火箭助推器的阿特拉斯V号火箭。是的，这是免费的照相馆。
烟和火..。由联合发射联盟制造的火箭在晴朗的天空下从卡纳维拉尔角空军基地起飞，我们将停止写作，因为这次史诗般的发射的照片不言而喻。我们不确定我们是否见过更令人惊叹的阿特拉斯5号发射。
星际飞船原型首次飞行。周二晚上，在太阳沉入德克萨斯州起伏的地平线前一个多小时，这艘星际飞船原型隆隆作响，首次离开地面。它从看台上升起，它的单个猛禽火箭发动机燃烧得干干净净。据Ars报道，然后这辆30米高的车辆上升到约150米，横向移动，在安全着陆之前开始下降。这个原型缺少关键的结构元素，包括大鼻锥、襟翼、级间等等。但关键的是，这辆车装有星际飞船的推进系统。
今年晚些时候会有更大的跳跃吗？…。在周二的测试之后，该计划的下一步行动尚不清楚。星期二&#39；的车辆是序列号5，或SN5。它现在可能要退役了。在德克萨斯州南部的帐篷和高位海湾内，SpaceX有SN6和SN8的组件，SN6是一种使用新钢材合金的独立探路坦克。后一种飞行器可能有一个鼻锥整流罩和飞行控制表面，它可能会将猛禽发动机的数量增加到三个。这款飞行器可能会在今年晚些时候进行长达20公里的试飞。
8月7日：火箭3.1|阿斯特拉助推器第二次发射尝试|阿拉斯加科迪亚克岛|世界协调时02：00。
8月7日：猎鹰9号|星联9号任务|佛罗里达州肯尼迪航天中心。|格林尼治标准时间05：12。
8月14日：阿丽亚娜5号|Galaxy 30，MEV-2和Amp；BSAT-4B卫星|法属圭亚那库鲁|世界协调时21：33
note_en:
Welcome to Edition 3.11 of the Rocket Report! A lot of the most interesting news this week came in the world of small launch, with Electron announcing a quick return to flight as well as boosting the capacity of its Electron booster. We were also surprised to see such a robust fundraising effort by ABL Space Systems.
 As always, we  welcome reader submissions, and if you don't want to miss an issue, please subscribe using the box below (the form will not appear on AMP-enabled versions of the site). Each report will include information on small-, medium-, and heavy-lift rockets as well as a quick look ahead at the next three launches on the calendar.
  Astra attempts launch of second orbital rocket. The  launch window for launching Rocket 3.1 from the company's spaceport on Kodiak Island, Alaska, opened Sunday night. A combination of technical issues with the rocket and ground systems, as well as weather issues, precluded launches on Sunday through Wednesday.
 Slipping toward the end of the window ... The company is now aiming to fly on Thursday, August 6, with the window opening at 7pm PST (02:00 UTC Friday). The goal for this flight, which will carry no commercial payload, is to get through the first-stage firing. Lighting the second stage and getting into orbit will be gravy, company CEO Chris Kemp told reporters last week. We wish Astra well. (submitted by Ken the Bin)
 Virgin Galactic delays commercial flights to 2021. Virgin Galactic has pushed the beginning of commercial flights of its SpaceShipTwo suborbital vehicle to no earlier than the first quarter of 2021 while announcing plans to sell additional stock to raise money,  SpaceNews reports. The company, in its fiscal second-quarter financial results released Monday, said it expected to perform two more test flights of SpaceShipTwo from Spaceport America in New Mexico, both of which will be powered flights.
 And then, the knight ... "Presuming things go as expected on this fully-crewed flight, we would then plan to fly Sir Richard Branson on the third powered flight from New Mexico," said George T. Whitesides, the company's chief space officer. This would mark the beginning of commercial service. At this point, we're in the we'll-believe-it-when-we-see-it camp on Virgin Galactic's plans for commercial space tourism. (submitted by JohnCarter17)
 The easiest way to keep up with Eric Berger's space reporting is to sign up for his newsletter, we'll collect his stories in your inbox.
 Rocket Lab announces return to flight this month. In a fairly quick turnaround, Rocket Lab says it has received approval from the Federal Aviation Administration to resume launches this month after identifying an anomalous electrical connection as the cause of an in-flight failure on July 4, 2020. The investigation was able to confidently narrow the issue down to a single anomalous electrical connection.
 Targeting August return-to-flight mission ... "The issue occurred under incredibly specific and unique circumstances, causing the connection to fail in a way that we wouldn't detect with standard testing," Rocket Lab chief Peter Beck  said. "Our team has now reliably replicated the issue in test and identified that it can be mitigated through additional testing and procedures." Separately, Rocket Lab  also announced an impressive increase in Electron's payload capacity from 225kg to 300kg to low Earth orbit. (submitted by trimeta, Unrulycow, Ken the Bin and platykurtic)
 ABL secures new funding. ABL Space Systems, a three-year-old startup developing a launch vehicle for small satellites, announced this week that it has received two US Air Force contracts worth $44.5 million, and secured $49 million in new private funding. ABL says it is planning the first orbital launch of its RS1 vehicle in 2021.
 On fundraising during a pandemic ... "We did close the round during the COVID-19 pandemic. No doubt there have been serious challenges in many people's lives this year, but I think the rhetoric around small launch funding being completely frozen and necessitating industry-level bailouts is a bit exaggerated," founder and CFO Dan Piemont  told SpaceNews. This is a good amount of money, and the company's technology seems to be maturing. We'll be eager to see where ABL goes. (submitted by JohnCarter17 and Ken the Bin)
 Virgin Orbit will carry NASA payloads on second launch. In a  lengthy blog post, Virgin Orbit said it had determined the root cause of its first launch-attempt failure: "A breach in the high-pressure line carrying cryogenic liquid oxygen to our first stage combustion chamber due to a component failure. Without a supply of oxidizer, that engine soon stopped providing thrust, ending our powered flight and ultimately the test itself."
 Updating second Launch Demo mission ... The company says flight hardware is in "final integration" now and will soon be shipped to Mojave, California, for tests. Notably, for the second mission, NASA has agreed to launch 11 CubeSats for its CubeSat Launch Initiative. This shows an admirable risk tolerance on NASA's part and some confidence on Virgin's part. The company plans to conduct this flight "towards the end of this year."
  Ariane 5 rocket now targeting August 14. On July 31, with less than three minutes to go in the countdown, the launch of an Ariane 5 rocket carrying three satellites was called to a halt. The reason? A technical issue involving "unexpected behavior" of a sensor inside the liquid-hydrogen tank of the main stage.
 Return from the pandemic ... Following this, the rocket was transferred back into the Final Assembly Building at the launch site in Kourou, French Guiana. This week,  Arianespace announced a new launch date for the mission, August 14, "with the possibility of an optimization in the process for the mission to be performed one day earlier." This is an important mission and the first to fly from Kourou since the spaceport shut down earlier this year due to COVID-19. (submitted by Ken the Bin)
 After delays, Starlink mission returns to launch pad. Following a six-week delay for undisclosed reasons, SpaceX raised a Falcon 9 vertical on its launch pad Thursday at the Kennedy Space Center in Florida,  Spaceflight Now reports. The company will try again early Friday to launch the company's next batch of Starlink Internet relay stations and a pair of commercial BlackSky Earth-imaging microsatellites.
 Late night launch ... Liftoff is set for 05:12 UTC Friday. This will be SpaceX's first launch to carry a full set of Starlink satellites equipped with new sunshades, or visors, in an attempt to make the spacecraft less visible to ground-based telescopes, addressing concerns voiced by astronomers that thousands of Starlink satellites could interfere with scientific observations.
 South Korea mulls exiting its own launch program. South Korean defense industry analysts say the nation would have difficulty competing with other countries in the launch industry, which can provide more advanced technologies and services at lower prices with their already established space-industry infrastructure. "Even if South Korea develops its own rockets, they would not be able to excel more than aerospace giants like SpaceX in terms of technology," said Shin Jong-woo, a senior researcher at the Korea Defense and Security Forum, in  The Korea Times.
 Ten times the cost ... The country's space institute, KARI, has developed several small-satellite rockets, but these are not competitive with SpaceX's Falcon 9, which recently launched South Korea's first military communications satellite, ANASIS-II. "The costs spent by the South Korean government to develop the two rockets were 10 times higher than those spent by SpaceX," Kim Seung-jo, former president of KARI, said. "But the South Korean government's outcomes in its rocket projects were far behind the Falcon 9 rocket produced by SpaceX." (submitted by JohnCarter17)
 SES selects SpaceX, ULA for commercial launches. In separate news releases, SES announced deals with America's two leading launch companies.  SpaceX will provide launch capability for up to three of its C-band satellites over two Falcon 9 launches, and  one United Launch Alliance Atlas V will carry the two stacked satellites. Only US rockets were considered for the commercial missions.
 No Vulcan for this one ... The launch of the C-band satellites will facilitate the distribution of the 5G network across the United States. The announcement is notable for a couple of reasons: first of all, it's rare for ULA to win a purely commercial contract. Secondly, the ULA mission will fly on an Atlas V, rather than a Vulcan rocket. This may indicate some lack of confidence on SES' behalf about the readiness of Vulcan in 2022. (submitted by JohnCarter17 and Ken the Bin)
 Swarm to launch on a Falcon 9 rideshare. Swarm Technologies is working with Exolaunch of Germany to send 24 SpaceBee satellites into orbit on the SpaceX Falcon 9 small-satellite ride-share mission scheduled to launch in December. Exolaunch, a ride-share launch and satellite-deployment company, will handle launch, integration, and deployment of SpaceBee satellites in sun-synchronous orbit,  SpaceNews reports.
 Targeting December ... Earlier this year, Exolaunch announced an agreement with SpaceX to send multiple small satellites into orbit on the December ride-share flight. For the December launch, Exolaunch plans to integrate microsatellites and cubesats on a Falcon 9 Evolved Expendable Launch Vehicle Secondary Payload Adapter port and to send the satellites into orbit with EXOpod, the firm's proprietary deployment system. (submitted by Ken the Bin and JohnCarter17)
 The Atlas V launch to Mars sure was gorgeous. Last Thursday, an Atlas V rocket launched NASA's latest rover, Perseverance, to the Red Planet. For this mission, NASA chose an Atlas V rocket with four solid rocket boosters.  Yes, this is a gratuitous photo gallery.
 Smoke and fire ... The rocket built by United Launch Alliance took flight under clear skies from Cape Canaveral Air Force Station, and, well, we'll stop writing because the photos of this epic launch speak for themselves. We're not sure we've ever seen a more breathtaking Atlas V launch.
  Starship prototype makes its first flight. On Tuesday evening, a little more than an hour before the Sun sank into the rolling Texas horizon, this Starship prototype rumbled to life and left the ground for the first time. It rose from a stand, with its single Raptor rocket engine burning clean. Then the 30-meter-tall vehicle ascended to about 150 meters, moved laterally, and began to descend before safely landing,  Ars reports. This prototype lacked key structural elements, including a large nose cone, flaps, an interstage, and more. But critically, this vehicle contained Starship's propulsion system.
 Larger hop late this year? ... After Tuesday's test, the program's next steps are unclear. Tuesday's vehicle was Serial Number 5, or SN5. It probably will now be retired. Inside the tents and high bays in South Texas, SpaceX has components for SN6, a separate pathfinder tank that uses a new steel alloy, and SN8. This latter vehicle is likely to have a nosecone fairing and flight-control surfaces, and it will probably increase the number of Raptor engines to three. This vehicle might possibly make a test flight up to 20km later this year.
  Aug. 7: Rocket 3.1 | Second launch attempt of Astra booster | Kodiak Island, Alaska | 02:00 UTC
 Aug. 7: Falcon 9 | Starlink-9 mission | Kennedy Space Center, Fla. | 05:12 UTC
 Aug. 14: Ariane 5 | Galaxy 30, MEV-2 & BSAT-4B satellites | Kourou, French Guiana | 21:33 UTC
title: 火箭报道：韩国SpaceX进退两难，火箭实验室找到捷径
title_en: Rocket Report: South Korea’s SpaceX dilemma, Rocket Lab finds a quick fix

note:
随着新冠肺炎疫情的规模和威胁变得清晰起来，追踪疾病传播的研究人员相当一致：为了为我们争取时间开发治疗或疫苗，各国需要实施严厉的限制措施，以限制病毒传播的机会。专家们描绘了巨大的感染高峰的可怕画面，如果封锁不到位，这些感染将使当地医院系统不堪重负，导致许多不必要的死亡。对于意大利和西班牙这样的国家来说，它们已经处于不受控制的蔓延的阵痛中，现实证明了这些预测是正确的。限制措施出台前，峰值大幅上升，但一旦实施，峰值几乎同样大幅下降。
但这些模型也预测，结束限制将使各国面临病毒几个月后卷土重来的风险，迫使各国政府在一个循环的下一步再次做出决定，要么严格限制，要么爆发失控的大流行，这个循环将重复下去，直到有疫苗或治疗方法可用。这些国家现在有一个有点不同的问题：有没有办法控制病毒，而不诉诸断断续续的封锁周期？对于美国这样的国家来说，如果病毒得到控制，同样的问题也会变得重要起来。美国曾短暂地、不稳定地、半心半意地实施了限制措施，以至于峰顶之间没有太多的低谷相隔。
一个大型国际研究小组的一项新研究使用流行病学模型来探索在允许大多数人恢复半正常生活的同时控制事态的方法。它发现，有一些方法可以处理放宽限制的问题，但这些方法需要有效的接触者追踪系统、广泛的测试和家庭愿意一起检疫的组合。
这项研究依赖于流行病学模型-在这种情况下，基础是一个相当标准的版本，将模型人群划分为易感、感染或康复的人群池。但这里使用的模型要复杂得多，这要归功于我们现在对SARS-CoV-2行为的更好理解。
正常情况下，易感人群通过与感染人群进行虚拟接触而受到感染。但他们没有直接进入受感染的池，而是经历了一段潜伏期，在这段潜伏期内，他们还没有传染性。一些人退出潜伏期进入无症状感染，这仍然允许他们传播病毒，直到他们到达恢复的池。其他人则走不同的道路，通过症状前但感染阶段，通过症状和感染期，建模为具有严重程度，可能包括住院和/或在重症监护病房花费的时间。这使得研究人员可以跟踪给定水平的感染会给卫生系统带来的菌株类型。
为了模拟可能使病毒传播的那种交互作用，研究人员利用了现实世界数据的一个关键来源：匿名手机数据。在这种情况下，他们有来自波士顿地区的6个月的大流行前手机位置数据，这些数据与人口普查数据一起使用。这使得他们能够产生一个三层的互动模型，跟踪合住房屋的人、工作或社交互动的人以及上学的人的感染机会。
为了为他们的模型提供基线，研究人员在没有任何限制的情况下运行了它。与其他不太复杂的模型一样，它产生了一个巨大的感染高峰，大大超出了医院处理患者的能力。在高峰期，典型的感染者会将病毒传染给易感人群中的四个人。到事情放慢的时候，75%的人口已经被感染了。
研究人员随后转向关注限制开始放松后会发生什么。他们运行该模型的情景是，在为期8周的在家工作订单之后，重新开放工作场所四周，然后取消大多数限制，包括对餐馆和剧院的限制。然而，学校和大学仍然关闭。这里的模型成功地匹配了纽约市发生的情况，就人们通常每天在不同限制下接触的数量而言。
但这也给纽约市和其他地方带来了坏消息：在全面取消限制之后，感染人数出现了非常大的高峰。这在大多数地方都没有发生，部分原因是许多工作场所没有重新开业，许多人已经采取了额外的措施，比如使用口罩，以限制这种流行病的传播。但它确实表明了完全重新开放的风险，特别是在其他措施无法保证的地方。虽然病例的激增只有模型显示的如果我们什么都不做的话我们会看到的一半左右，但它仍然很容易让医疗系统不堪重负。
但研究人员的主要关注点是一种处理重新开放的方法，他们认为这种方法可以极大地降低风险。它需要一个足够大的检测能力，以便大约一半的有症状的新冠肺炎病例可以在症状出现后的两天内被识别出来。一旦确认，这些人将在家中隔离两周。关键的是，任何通常与该人住在一起的人也必须在家中隔离两周-本质上，为了隔离的目的，它将家庭视为单个单位。研究人员计划的另一个关键是，接触者追踪足够有效，可以追踪到与那些检测呈阳性的人有合理比例的接触(他们尝试的值从追踪到的接触者的20%到40%不等)，并将这些人隔离。
总体而言，这似乎相当合理。这并不要求我们完全有效地识别无症状病例或有症状的人。它也不指望高水平的成功追踪接触者，这可能需要大多数人在他们的智能手机上安装接触者追踪应用程序。但也需要承认的是，美国的测试能力远远不足以使这在今天成为现实。
这个模型的结果是相当戏剧性的。即使在20%的成功追踪接触者的情况下，新感染的第二波高峰也下降到了原来的20%左右，而且仍然在医院系统的能力范围内。如果接触者追踪达到40%，那么第二波基本上就消除了，只会比背景情况略有逐渐上升。虽然检疫限制看起来很严格，但研究人员发现，在这股浪潮的高峰期，只有9%的人口必须接受检疫。
这个系统也允许一定程度的灵活性。如果我们在识别感染者方面做得更好，我们就能逃脱一部分违反检疫规定的家庭的惩罚。拥有定期检查阳性病例所在家庭的情况的检测能力，可以实现更灵活的检疫。研究小组还对开放的学校和大学进行了建模，发现我们可以管理这一举措，并通过将接触者追踪的效率提高到50%来控制大流行。
作者还表明，关键的额外安全措施，如口罩的使用，提高了这一退出策略的有效性，使我们能够在其他步骤效率较低的情况下逃脱惩罚。
这一点也很清楚，这种方法实际上可以在现实世界中试用。有大量国家的检测能力足够高，可以确保快速识别病例，而接触者追踪可能有效的病例却很少。对于像美国这样的国家(两者都不是这样)，它提供了指导，指导各州和城市如何设定目标，然后才能放松限制，以及如何按原样行事。
更广泛地说，关注如何有效地退出封锁标志着卫生专家对这场大流行的看法发生了关键转变。运行最初的模型只是为了了解不受控制的大流行的潜在后果，它们表明我们将面临感染激增的一年，然后是停摆-这显然是一个令人沮丧的未来。然而，随着时间的推移，研究转向试图找出如何以一种降低未来激增风险的方式退出封锁。现在，我们正处在这样一个时刻，模型被用来确定对社会施加最宽松限制的方法，同时仍然控制住大流行。
关键问题仍然是，当公共卫生专家指出走出当前困境的最安全途径时，我们是否能让社会信任他们。
note_en:
As the scale and threat of the COVID-19 pandemic became clear, researchers who trace the spread of diseases were pretty unanimous: to buy us time to develop a therapy or vaccine, countries needed to implement heavy-handed restrictions to limit the opportunities for the virus to spread. Experts  painted frightening pictures of huge peaks of infections that would overwhelm local hospital systems if lockdowns weren't put in place, leading to many unnecessary deaths. For countries like Italy and Spain, which were already in the throes of an uncontrolled spread, reality bore these predictions out. Peaks rose sharply in advance of restrictions but fell nearly as sharply once they were put in place.
 But those same models also predicted that ending the restrictions would put countries at risk of a return of the virus a few months later, forcing governments to again decide between strict restrictions or an out-of-control pandemic in the next step of a cycle that would repeat until a vaccine or therapy became available. Those countries now have a somewhat different question: are there ways of controlling the virus without resorting to a cycle of on-and-off lockdowns? For countries like the US, which implemented restrictions briefly, erratically, and half heartedly, such that peaks haven't been separated by much of a trough, the same question will become relevant if we ever get the virus under control.
  A new study by a large international team uses epidemiological models to explore ways of keeping things in check while allowing most of the population to resume a semi-normal life. It finds that there are ways of handling restriction easing, but they require a combination of an effective contact tracing system, extensive testing, and a willingness of households to quarantine together.
  The study relied on  an epidemiological model—in this case, the basis was a fairly standard version that splits up the model population into pools of people who are susceptible, infected, or recovered. But the model used here was significantly more complex, thanks to the much better understanding we now have of SARS-CoV-2's behavior.
  As normal, people in the susceptible pool become infected by coming into virtual contact with someone in the infected pool. But instead of going directly into the infected pool, they then experience a period of latency, in which they're not yet infectious. Some people exit latency into an asymptomatic infection, which still allows them to transmit the virus until they reach the recovered pool. Others follow a different path through a pre-symptomatic but infectious phase, through a symptomatic-and-infectious period that's modeled to have a severity that could include time spent hospitalized and/or in an intensive care unit. This allows the researchers to track the sort of strain a given level of infection would put on the health system.
 To model the sort of interactions that could enable the virus to spread, the researchers took advantage of a key source of real-world data: anonymized cell phone data. In this case, they had six months of pre-pandemic cell phone location data from the Boston area, which they used in conjunction with census data. This allowed them to generate a three-layered model of interactions, tracking infection opportunities at the level of people who shared housing, those who interacted at work or socially, and those who went to school.
 To provide a baseline for their model, the researchers ran it without any restrictions in place. As with other, less sophisticated models, it produced a huge peak of infections that greatly exceeded the capacity of hospitals to handle patients. At its peak, the typical infected person passed the virus on to four people in the susceptible pool. By the time things slow down, 75 percent of the population had been infected.
  The researchers then switched to focusing on what happens after restrictions start being eased. They ran the model in a scenario in which an eight-week-long stay-at-home order was followed by reopening workplaces for four weeks, before most restrictions, including those on restaurants and theaters, are lifted. Schools and universities, however, remain closed. The model here successfully matched what has happened in New York City in terms of the number of contacts people typically have each day under the different restrictions.
 But it also has bad news for New York City and elsewhere: a very large peak of infections happening in the wake of the full lifting of restrictions. This hasn't happened in most places, in part because many workplaces haven't reopened, and many people have adopted additional measures, such as face mask use, that limit the pandemic's spread. But it does indicate the risks of a complete reopening, especially in places where the other measures can't be guaranteed. While the surge in cases is only about half of what the model indicates we'd see if we did nothing, it's still easily enough to overwhelm healthcare systems.
 But the researchers' main focus was on a way of handling reopening that they argue could greatly reduce the risk. It requires a testing capacity that's large enough that about half the symptomatic COVID-19 cases can be identified within two days of the onset of symptoms. Once identified, these people quarantine at home for two weeks. Critically, anyone who normally lives with that person also has to quarantine at home for two weeks—essentially, it treats households as single units for the purposes of quarantine. Another key to the researchers' plan is that contact tracing is efficient enough to track down a reasonable percentage of the people who have been in proximity to those who get a positive test (they try values ranging from 20 percent to 40 percent of contacts traced) and that said individuals go into quarantine.
 Overall, this seems pretty reasonable. It doesn't demand that we identify asymptomatic cases or the people with symptoms with complete efficiency. It also doesn't expect high levels of successful contact tracing, which would probably require most of the population to install contact tracing applications on their smartphones. But it also needs to be acknowledged that the US' testing capabilities are nowhere near good enough to make this a reality today.
 The results in this model are pretty dramatic. Even at 20-percent successful contact tracing, the "second wave" peak of new infections drops to about 20 percent of the size it would be otherwise, and it remains within the capacity of hospital systems. If contact tracing reaches 40 percent, then the second wave is essentially eliminated, with only a slight and gradual rise above background cases. And while the quarantine restrictions seem severe, the researchers find that at the wave's peak, only nine percent of the population has to be in quarantine.
  This system also allows for a certain degree of flexibility. If we're better at identifying infected people, we could get away with some fraction of the households breaking quarantine. Having the testing capacity to regularly check the status of those in the same household as a positive case could allow a more flexible quarantine. The research team also modeled opening schools and universities and found that we could manage that move and keep the pandemic in check by increasing the efficiency of contact tracing to 50 percent.
 The authors also show that key additional safety measures, like face mask use, improve the effectiveness of this exit strategy, allowing us to get away with lower efficiency at other steps.
 It's also clear that the approach can actually be tried in the real world. There are a large number of countries where the testing capacity is high enough to ensure that cases get identified quickly, and few enough cases that contact tracing is likely to be effective. For countries like the US, where neither is the case, it provides guidance for how states and cities might set goals to reach before restrictions can be eased, and what to do as they are.
 More generally, the focus on how to effectively exit lockdowns marks a critical transition in how health experts are thinking about the pandemic. The initial models were run simply to understand the potential consequences of an uncontrolled pandemic, and they suggested we were in for a year of surges in infections, followed by lockdowns—an obviously depressing future. Over time, however, research shifted into trying to figure out how to exit the lockdowns in a way that reduces the risk of future surges. Now, we're at the point where models are being used to identify the approaches that put the lightest restrictions on society while still keeping the pandemic in check.
 The key question will remain whether we can get society to trust the public health experts when they indicate the safest paths out of the current mess.
title: 新的研究模型显示了从大流行封锁中走出来的方法
title_en: New study models ways of emerging from a pandemic lockdown

随着世界各地办公室的关闭，许多人第一次体验到在家工作-或者体验的时间比过去长得多。许多公司计划让员工在2021年之前至少在部分时间内远程工作。一些人正在考虑将其永久化。
不计其数的人不得不即兴搭建他们的在家工作空间。但现在我们已经过了几个月了，其中一些即兴表演可能会变得乏善可陈。在所有这些即兴创作中，有一件事经常被放在次要位置，那就是人体工程学。如果你过去没有经常在家工作，而现在你每天都坐在厨房的桌子旁，用公司发行的笔记本电脑工作，那么你很可能正感受到这一不同寻常的现实给你带来的身体上的压力。(注：如果你过去不经常在家工作，而现在你每天都坐在厨房桌子旁用公司提供的笔记本电脑工作)，那么你很可能正感受到这一不同寻常的现实带来的身体压力。
作为一个主要在家工作了四分之一个世纪的人，我有很多时间来弄清楚什么可以在家庭办公室工作，什么不可以。新冠肺炎带来的变化-包括让我的妻子和女儿和我一起禁闭，两人都在家里工作-需要一些调整和一些重新装备。我们需要我们的家庭工作空间来支持新的工作世界，同时在大流行期间保持舒适和合理的理智水平。
现在不是开始的理想时间，但关注家庭工作空间的人体工程学仍然很重要。你现在最不想做的事就是给生活增加更多的压力(以及潜在的重复性压力伤害)。这可能意味着购买一些新的东西来装饰你的工作空间，或者即兴使用你所拥有的东西来使在家工作更可持续(或者至少不违反OSHA)。
并不是每个人都能负担得起家里有专门的工作空间。但是，在家里挑一个特别是工作区的地方，并确保它能满足一些基本需求，这会很有帮助。确保光线均匀，以减少眼睛疲劳，特别是当你将眼睛从电脑显示器移到其他地方时。如果你在一个没有顶灯的房间里，在你的办公桌附近增加一些任务照明是至关重要的--尤其是在你的笔记本电脑屏幕后面。
普遍接受的办公室照明标准是每平方米500流明(勒克斯)，或略高于45英尺蜡烛。OSHA对室内办公室的最低允许照明是30英尺蜡烛(322勒克斯)。考虑到一个60瓦的灯泡大约能产生800流明，如果你有一个120平方英尺(11平方米)的办公室，你需要5个均匀分布的60瓦灯泡(产生4000流明)才能达到OSHA的最低要求。
当我重新装备我的家庭办公室时，我装上了LED天花板照明，借助窗户的自然光，它能很好地照亮我的工作空间。但是，一旦我妻子开始在那里呆上一整天，我们就需要为她的工作空间安装一些任务照明设备。任务照明是最快、最经济的选择-一盏定向照明的落地灯，使用明亮的LED灯泡(与一些现有灯泡的新灯泡相结合)，将她工作空间周围的光线带出了黑暗时代。台灯可以很好，但这取决于您想要放弃多少桌面空间来照明。
你选择的照明在很大程度上取决于什么能让你感觉更有效率和更放松。虽然我喜欢日光白色，但你可能喜欢暖和一点的。我出于各种原因对物联网灯泡产生反感，但它们的优势之一是很多(包括Wyze和飞利浦色调)都可以调到大范围的色温。我设置了飞利浦色调灯，这样我就可以根据一天中的时间和任务来改变照明的温度。
沙发不适合做一个好办公室。不管他们怎么称呼它，“笔记本电脑”不会在你的腿上放很长时间。不，你不会(必然)不育，也没有证据表明Wi-Fi会让你得腿癌。这更多的是关于姿势和重复的压力-你的脖子和手腕比你的生殖器面临更大的风险。即使是那些专为坐在沙发上或其他地方使用而设计的笔记本电脑“办公桌”，也不足以纠正在一件专为放松而设计的家具上工作所造成的姿势恐惧。
长时间坐在餐桌前工作也是如此：那把椅子和那张桌子不是为长时间打字而设计的。当我在办公室里等待油漆干燥时，我在餐厅工作了一天--但这并不是最理想的。
问题不一定在桌子上。是椅子，还有桌子的相对高度。梅奥诊所关于人体工程学的建议是，工作面在肘部高度或以下，屏幕与之相隔一臂长，并以轻微向下的角度观看。如果你是坐着的，梅奥诊所说你的膝盖应该与臀部齐平，所以桌子下面应该有足够的间隙来放置膝盖、大腿和脚。你还需要足够好的腰部支撑，以确保你坐在与臀部成90度角的位置，而不是弓着身子向前或向后倾。
我之所以说“如果你坐着”，是因为我很久以前就放弃了办公椅，转而使用全职站立式办公桌。1995年，我的雇主为我的第一次全职家庭办公室工作购买的副翼座椅使用年限结束后，我不愿自己花那么多钱买一把新椅子--特别是以记者的预算。但我发现，便宜的办公椅不能提供足够的腰部支撑，而且这些选项也不太适合我的多显示器、多键盘的工作方式。最终，我发现如果我整天坐着，我就是活动不足。
因为我的身高，我发现大多数可转换的桌子(包括那些只需按一下按钮就能站起来的电动桌子)都不够高，不能舒适地打字，我对它们支撑着我的一系列显示器持谨慎态度。我最终买了一个赫斯基工作台和一把可调的绘图椅，供偶尔休息。(从人体工程学角度来看，全天站立也不是一个很好的选择。)。
当我妻子开始在家工作时，我说服她，选择站立是个好主意。所以我给她买了一个Flexispot笔记本电脑办公桌立管，放在她的桌子上，腿长可调。然后我为我们两个人买了平衡板，以便在一天中增加一些额外的运动。
至于办公椅，你应该寻求支撑和舒适。如果你考虑的是长远，那就准备付钱吧：赫尔曼·米勒(Herman Miller)和斯蒂尔凯斯(Steelcase)符合人体工程学的椅子舒适、坚固、适合大量使用，价格超过1,000美元。WORKPRO 12000系列以不到500美元的价格提供了许多副翼的优点-透气的背部和良好的腰部支撑。
不过，如果你看到自己在这一切结束后回到办公室通勤，你可能会考虑一把组合式办公室/游戏椅-例如，有点像GTP Office的游戏椅。这是一把很好的两用椅子，可以在任何一种屏幕前工作几个小时，而且它有一个腰部按摩功能，对于我们正在经历的所有非工作压力来说，这可能会派上用场。
note_en:
With offices shuttered around the world, many people are experiencing working from home for the first time—or experiencing it in  much longer doses than they were used to. Many companies are planning to keep employees working remotely at least part of the time well into 2021. And some are considering making it permanent.
  Countless people have had to improvise their work-at-home workspaces. But now that we're several months in, some of that improvisation may be wearing thin. And one of the things that often gets pushed to the back burner in all this improvisation is ergonomics. If you haven't worked from home regularly in the past, and you're now sitting at the kitchen table every day working from a corporate-issued laptop, you're probably feeling the physical strains of this never-going-to-be-normal reality.
 As someone who has worked primarily from home for a quarter of a century, I've had a lot of time to figure out what does and does not work in a home office. The changes that have come with COVID-19—including having my wife and daughter in lockdown with me, both working from home themselves—have required some adjustments and some re-equipping. We needed our home workspaces to support the new world of work while maintaining comfort and a reasonable level of sanity mid-pandemic.
  These are not ideal times to begin with, but it’s still important to pay attention to the ergonomics of your home workspace. The last thing you want to do is add more stress (and potential repetitive stress injury) to life right now. That may mean acquiring a few new things to furnish your workspace, or improvising with what you have to make working from home more sustainable (or at least less of an OSHA violation).
  Not everyone can afford a dedicated space in their home for work. But it helps to pick someplace in your home that is specifically a workspace and to make sure it meets some basic needs. Make sure you’ve got even lighting to reduce eye strain, especially as you move your eyes from the computer monitor to elsewhere. If you’re in a room where there’s no overhead lighting, it's crucial to add some task lighting near your desk—especially behind your laptop screen.
  The generally accepted  standard for office lighting is 500 lumens per square meter (lux), or just over 45 foot-candles.  OSHA’s minimum allowable lighting for indoor offices is 30 foot-candles (322 lux). Given that a 60-watt bulb produces about 800 lumens, if you’ve got a 120 square foot (11 square meter) office, you would need five evenly spaced 60-watt bulbs (generating 4,000 lumens) just to meet OSHA’s minimum.
 When I re-outfitted my home office, I put in  LED ceiling lighting that does a good job illuminating my workspace with help from natural light from windows. But we needed to get some task lighting for my wife’s workspace once she started spending the whole day there. Task lighting was the quickest and most economical option—a floor lamp with directional lighting, using a bright LED bulb (in combination with some new bulbs for existing lights), brought the light around her workspace out of the dark ages. Desk lamps can be good, but it depends how much desk space you want to give up to lighting.
  The lighting you choose will depend greatly on what makes you feel more productive and relaxed. While I’ve gone for daylight white, you may prefer something warmer. I have an aversion to Internet of Things lightbulbs for various reasons, but one of their advantages is that many (including  Wyze and  Philips Hue) are  tunable to a wide range of color temperatures. I have Philips Hue lights set up so that I can change the temperature of lighting depending on time of day and task.
  The couch does not make a good office. Regardless of what they call it, a “laptop” does not belong on your lap for very long. No, you’re not going to (necessarily) go infertile, and there’s no evidence to suggest that Wi-Fi is going to give you leg cancer. It’s more about posture and repetitive stress—your neck and wrists are at greater risk than your genitals. Even those laptop “desks” designed for use while sitting on the couch or elsewhere do not correct enough for the posture horrors created by working on a piece of furniture designed for relaxation.
 The same goes for working from the dining room table for a prolonged period of time: that chair and that table are not designed for hours of typing. While I was waiting for the paint to dry in my office, I worked from the dining room for a day—and it was less than optimal.
  It’s not necessarily the table that’s the problem. It’s the chair, along with the relative  height of the table. The  Mayo Clinic’s recommendations on ergonomics are for a work surface that is at or below elbow height, with a screen that’s an arm’s length away—and viewed at a slight downward angle. If you’re sitting, the Mayo Clinic says your knees should be about level with your hips, so there should be adequate clearance under the desk for knees, thighs and feet. You’ll also need good enough lumbar support to make sure you’re sitting at about a 90-degree angle to your hips, not hunched forward or leaning back.
 I say “if you’re sitting” because I gave up on office chairs a long time ago and moved to a full-time standing desk. After my Aeron chair my employers bought for my first full-time home office gig in 1995 reached the end of its useful life, I was reluctant to shell out that kind of money on my own for a new chair—particularly on a journalist’s budget. But I found that cheaper office chairs didn’t offer enough lumbar support, and those options didn’t fit in well with my multi-monitor, multi-keyboard work style. And ultimately, I found that if I saw sitting all day, I just wasn’t moving around enough.
 Because of my height, I found most convertible desks (including the electrically powered ones that rise to standing height with a push of a button) were not tall enough to comfortably type at, and I was leery about them supporting my array of monitors. I eventually bought a  Husky workbench and an  adjustable drafting chair for occasional resting periods. (Standing all day is not a great ergonomic choice, either.)
  When my wife started working from home, I convinced her that having a choice about standing was a good idea. So I got her a  Flexispot Laptop desk riser to go on top of her desk with adjustable-length legs. Then I purchased  balance boards for both of us to add some additional motion into the day.
 As far as office chairs go, you should go for support and comfort. If you’re thinking long-term, prepare to pay for it:  Herman Miller and  Steelcase ergonomic chairs are comfortable, sturdy, made for heavy use, and cost over $1,000. The  WorkPro 12000 Series offers many of the advantages of the Aeron—a breathable back, and good lumbar support—for under $500.
 If you see yourself going back to an office commute when all this is over, though, you may consider a combo office/gaming chair—something like  GTP Office’s gaming chair, for example. That's a good dual-use chair for hours of either kind of screen time, and it has a lumbar massage feature that could come in handy with all the non-work stress we’re experiencing.
title: 草坪椅和餐桌：无意识在家工作时代的人体工程学
title_en: Lawn chairs and kitchen tables: Ergonomics in the involuntary work-from-home era

TikTok威胁要就唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)总统禁止美国公司和个人与母公司ByteDance做生意的行政命令起诉特朗普政府。

TikTok在周五发布的一份声明中表示：我们对最近的行政命令感到震惊，该命令在没有任何正当程序的情况下发布。
近一年来，我们一直寻求真诚地与美国政府接触，为表达的关切提供建设性的解决方案，它补充道。相反，我们遇到的是政府无视事实，在没有经过标准法律程序的情况下口述协议条款，并试图将自己插入私营企业之间的谈判。

我们明确表示，我们打算与适当的官员合作，设计一种解决方案，使我们的用户、创作者、合作伙伴、员工和更广泛的美国社区受益。过去没有，现在仍然没有正当程序，也没有遵守法律。该决定的文本清楚地表明，人们一直依赖未具名、没有引用的报道，担心这款应用可能被用于虚假信息宣传，而这种担忧没有得到证实，以及对收集数据的担忧，这是全球数千款移动应用的行业标准。我们已经明确表示，TikTok从未与中国政府共享用户数据，也从未应中国政府的要求审查内容。事实上，我们在我们的透明中心提供了我们的审核指导方针和算法源代码，这是任何同行公司都没有承诺的问责级别。我们甚至表示愿意寻求将美国业务全部出售给一家美国公司。

note_en:
TikTok has threatened to sue the Trump administration over President Donald Trump's executive order barring US companies and individuals from doing business with its parent company ByteDance.
 Trump  signed the executive order Thursday, arguing that the Chinese firm poses a national security risk.
 "We are shocked by the recent Executive Order, which was issued without any due process," TikTok said in a  statement published Friday.
 "For nearly a year, we have sought to engage with the US government in good faith to provide a constructive solution to the concerns that have been expressed," it added. "What we encountered instead was that the Administration paid no attention to facts, dictated terms of an agreement without going through standard legal processes, and tried to insert itself into negotiations between private businesses."
   TikTok suggested that the executive order — set to take effect in September — was illegal and that it might challenge the diktat in court.
 "We will pursue all remedies available to us in order to ensure that the rule of law is not discarded and that our company and our users are treated fairly — if not by the Administration, then by the US courts," the company said.
 This week reports emerged that the Trump administration was trying to force TikTok to sell off its US business or face a ban.  Microsoft has publicly announced it's pursuing acquisition talks for TikTok's US, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand businesses. Trump also issued a similar executive order on Thursday targeting the Tencent-owned WeChat.
 TikTok accused the Trump administration of flouting the law by ignoring due process. "This Executive Order risks undermining global businesses' trust in the United States' commitment to the rule of law, which has served as a magnet for investment and spurred decades of American economic growth," it said.
    "TikTok is a community full of creativity and passion, a home that brings joy to families and meaningful careers to creators. And we are building this platform for the long term. TikTok will be here for many years to come.
 "We are shocked by the recent Executive Order, which was issued without any due process. For nearly a year, we have sought to engage with the US government in good faith to provide a constructive solution to the concerns that have been expressed. What we encountered instead was that the Administration paid no attention to facts, dictated terms of an agreement without going through standard legal processes, and tried to insert itself into negotiations between private businesses.
 "We made clear our intentions to work with the appropriate officials to devise a solution to benefit our users, creators, partners, employees, and the broader community in the United States. There has been, and continues to be, no due process or adherence to the law. The text of the decision makes it plain that there has been a reliance on unnamed "reports" with no citations, fears that the app "may be" used for misinformation campaigns with no substantiation of such fears, and concerns about the collection of data that is industry standard for thousands of mobile apps around the world. We have made clear that TikTok has never shared user data with the Chinese government, nor censored content at its request. In fact, we make our moderation guidelines and algorithm source code available in our Transparency Center, which is a level of accountability no peer company has committed to. We even expressed our willingness to pursue a full sale of the US business to an American company.
 "This Executive Order risks undermining global businesses' trust in the United States' commitment to the rule of law, which has served as a magnet for investment and spurred decades of American economic growth. And it sets a dangerous precedent for the concept of free expression and open markets. We will pursue all remedies available to us in order to ensure that the rule of law is not discarded and that our company and our users are treated fairly – if not by the Administration, then by the US courts.
   "We want the 100 million Americans who love our platform because it is your home for expression, entertainment, and connection to know: TikTok has never, and will never, waver in our commitment to you. We prioritize your safety, security, and the trust of our community – always. As TikTok users, creators, partners, and family, you have the right to express your opinions to your elected representatives, including the White House. You have the right to be heard."
  By clicking ‘Sign up’, you agree to receive marketing emails from Business Insider as well as other partner offers and accept our  Terms of Service and  Privacy Policy.
        Two crossed lines that form an 'X'. It indicates a way to close an interaction, or dismiss a notification.
title: TikTok威胁要就行政命令起诉特朗普政府
title_en: TikTok threatens to sue Trump administration over executive order

note:
Facebook周四表示，它已经对罗马尼亚的一个巨魔农场进行了深度筛选，该农场充斥着伪装成支持特朗普总统的美国黑人的账户。
据NBC新闻报道，这些帖子据称出自一个名叫大卫·阿德里安(David Adrian)的人，他住在蒙大拿州，但不知何故也同时在罗马尼亚。但这个人是虚构的，有一张偷来的头像。据NBC新闻报道，Facebook、Instagram和Twitter都删除了账户。
这一披露是在Facebook每月发布的协调一致的不真实行为报告中披露的，该报告开始试图剔除可能扭曲选举和其他重要决策的错误信息。
该平台安全政策负责人纳撒尼尔·格莱彻(Nathaniel Gleicher)强调，这些账户被关闭是因为他们的行为，而不一定是他们的内容。Gleicher告诉最先报道这起事件的NBC新闻，动机尚不清楚。
Facebook表示：“我们删除了35个Facebook账户、3个页面和88个Instagram账户，因为它们违反了我们反对外国干预的政策，这是代表外国实体协调的不真实行为。”“这项活动起源于罗马尼亚，重点放在美国。”
Facebook表示，该网络背后的那些人使用虚假账户冒充美国人，包括一些冒充特朗普总统粉丝页面的人。
Facebook说：“这个网络发布了关于美国国内的新闻和事件，包括即将到来的11月大选，特朗普的竞选活动，以及非裔美国人对竞选活动的支持，保守的意识形态，基督教信仰和QAnon。”“他们还经常转发美国保守派新闻网和特朗普竞选团队的报道。”
QAnon是一个阴谋论网络，其信徒认为特朗普与“华盛顿邮报”(The Washington Post)所描述的崇拜撒旦、贩卖儿童、根深蒂固的国家邪教对立。
该平台称，这一活动是Facebook内部调查的一部分，调查对象是“在2020年美国大选前涉嫌协同不真实的行为”。“大约1600个账户关注了其中一个或多个页面，大约7200人关注了一个或多个Instagram账户。”
Facebook表示，除了这个相对较小的网络外，Facebook还删除了一系列其他账户、页面、群组和Instagram账户，这些账户、页面、群组和Instagram账户来自多个国家，活动与一家名为Truth Media的数字媒体机构有关。
据NBC新闻报道，这次行动与支持特朗普的媒体组织大纪元传媒集团(Epoch Media Group)有联系，大纪元时报的母公司。
据NBC新闻报道，Facebook删除了303个Facebook账户，181个页面，44个群组和31个Instagram账户，总共删除了200多万人，并与数字媒体Truth Media捆绑在一起，Truth Media经常宣传大纪元时报的内容，但Facebook及其附属平台禁止该媒体。
Gleicher告诉NBC新闻，这些账户发布了关于“美国正在进行的抗议活动和关于谁是幕后黑手的阴谋论”。他们中的一些人发布了冠状病毒的错误信息。
note_en:
Facebook said Thursday it had deep-sixed a Romania-based troll farm rife with accounts masquerading as Black Americans supporting President Trump.
  The posts purported to be from a guy named David Adrian, who lived in Montana but also somehow simultaneously in Romania, NBC News reported. But the person was fictitious, with a stolen profile photo. Facebook, Instagram and Twitter all removed accounts,  NBC News said.
    The disclosure came in Facebook’s monthly  Coordinated Inauthentic Behavior Report, begun to try and weed out misinformation that could skew elections and other important decisions.
  The platform’s head of security policy, Nathaniel Gleicher, emphasized that the accounts were shut down because of their behavior, not necessarily their content. The motive was not clear, Gleicher told NBC News, which first reported the takedown.
      “We removed 35 Facebook accounts, three pages and 88 Instagram accounts for violating our policy against foreign interference, which is coordinated inauthentic behavior on behalf of a foreign entity,” Facebook said. “This activity originated in Romania and focused on the U.S.”
  Those behind the network used fake accounts to pose as Americans, including some posing as President Trump fan pages, Facebook said.
      “This network posted about U.S. domestic news and events, including the upcoming November election, the Trump campaign and support for the campaign by African Americans, conservative ideology, Christian beliefs, and QAnon,” Facebook said. “They also frequently reposted stories by American conservative news networks and the Trump campaign.”
  QAnon is the conspiracy-theory network whose believers think Trump is pitted against a Satan-worshipping, child-trafficking, deep-state cult, as  The Washington Post described it.
    The activity was unearthed as part of Facebook’s internal investigation into “suspected coordinated inauthentic behavior ahead of the 2020 election in the U.S.,” the platform said. “About 1,600 accounts followed one or more of these pages, and around 7,200 people followed one or more of these Instagram accounts.”
    In addition to that relatively small network, Facebook took out a slew of other accounts, pages, groups and Instagram accounts that were operating from numerous countries with activity linked to a digital media outfit called Truth Media, Facebook said.
  This operation had links to the pro-Trump media organization Epoch Media Group, parent of The Epoch Times newspaper, NBC News reported.
  Facebook removed 303 Facebook accounts, 181 Pages, 44 Groups and 31 Instagram accounts, followed by more than 2 million people in all and tied to the digital media outlet Truth Media, which often boosts Epoch Times content but which is banned from Facebook and affiliated platforms, NBC News said.
    Those accounts posted about “ongoing U.S. protests and conspiracy theories about who is behind them,” Gleicher told NBC News. Some of them posted coronavirus misinformation.
title: Facebook抹去了罗马尼亚的巨魔账号农场，伪装成黑人特朗普的支持者
title_en: Facebook obliterates Romania-based troll farm of accounts masquerading as Black Trump supporters

note:
参议员伯尼·桑德斯(Bernie Sanders)共同发起的一项新法案将对亿万富翁在冠状病毒大流行期间积累的财富征收一次性暴利税，将所得用于支付所有美国人一年的免费自付医疗费用。
桑德斯和马萨诸塞州民主党参议员埃德·马基(Ed Markey)和纽约州民主党参议员基尔斯滕·吉利布兰德(Kirsten Gillibrand)周四提出的“让亿万富翁支付法案”(Make Billionaire Pay Act)将对美国467名最富有的人自3月18日以来积累的大约7318亿美元征收60%的税。在此期间，超过5000万美国人申请失业援助，540万人失去了医疗保险，国家经济陷入一团糟。
参议员们说，一次性税收的收入将允许联邦医疗保险在一年内支付每个美国人的所有自付医疗费用，包括处方药。
佛蒙特州无党派人士桑德斯在一份声明中表示，该法案将“对亿万富翁在这场非同寻常的危机中取得的淫秽财富收益征税”。
桑德斯说：“在经济遭受巨大痛苦和磨难的时候，我们必须做出根本性的选择。”“我们可以继续允许非常富有的人变得更加富有，而其他人却变得越来越穷。或者，我们可以对大流行期间少数亿万富翁的奖金征税，以改善数千万美国人的健康和福祉。“。
根据这份声明，税后最富有的0.001%的美国人今年仍将获得超过3,100亿美元的集体财富收益。
-亚马逊公司(Amazon.com Inc.)。亚马逊(AMZN)首席执行官杰夫·贝佐斯将一次性缴纳428亿美元的税款。贝佐斯是地球上最富有的人，他的财富在疫情期间增长了63%，达到1900亿美元以上。
-特斯拉公司(Tesla Inc.)。特斯拉首席执行官埃隆·马斯克(Elon Musk)将支付2,750万美元。近几个月来，随着特斯拉股价飙升，马斯克的财富几乎翻了两番。
-Facebook Inc.。FB，首席执行官马克·扎克伯格(Mark Zuckerberg)愿意支付228亿美元。在疫情爆发期间，扎克伯格的财富增加了近400亿美元。
-沃尔顿家族，沃尔玛公司的继承人。财富增长210亿美元的《财富》杂志(WMT)将支付129亿美元。
“令人难以置信的是，”参议员们在一份声明中说，“这些亿万富翁目前支付的实际税率低于教师或卡车司机。”
他们说，任何净资产低于10亿美元的人都不会缴纳这笔税。
吉利布兰德说：“要求亿万富翁支付他们的公平份额，将有助于支持应对失业、食品不安全、住房不稳定和医疗保健的工人和家庭。”“这不仅是一个常识性的提案，而且是一个道德提案，国会应该尽我们所能帮助正在苦苦挣扎的美国人。”
继特朗普2017年减税之后，对超级富豪征税已成为民主党人的普遍呼声。去年，马萨诸塞州民主党参议员伊丽莎白·沃伦(Elizabeth Warren)提议对“超级百万富翁”征税，作为她总统竞选的一部分。
应该指出的是，正如MarketWatch今年早些时候报道的那样，从3月18日开始计时缺乏一些背景，因为投资者从2月份开始担心流感大流行，导致股市暂时但大幅暴跌。
今年，道琼斯工业平均指数-0.31%下跌4%，标准普尔500指数-0.07%上涨近4%，纳斯达克综合指数-0.21%上涨24%。
note_en:
A new bill co-sponsored by Sen. Bernie Sanders would impose a one-time windfall tax on the wealth that billionaires have accumulated during the coronavirus pandemic, using the proceeds to pay for free out-of-pocket health-care costs for all Americans for a year.
  The  “Make Billionaires Pay Act,” introduced Thursday by Sanders and Democratic Sens. Ed Markey of Massachusetts and Kirsten Gillibrand of New York, would slap a 60% tax on the roughly $731.8 billion that the 467 richest people in America have accumulated since March 18 — a period in which more than 50 million Americans have applied for jobless aid, 5.4 million lost their health insurance and the nation’s economy has been thrown into shambles.
  Proceeds from the one-time tax would allow Medicare to cover all out-of-pocket health-care costs for every American, including prescription drugs, for one year, the senators said.
  In a statement, Sanders, the Vermont independent, said the bill would “tax the obscene wealth gains billionaires have made during this extraordinary crisis.”
    “At a time of enormous economic pain and suffering, we have a fundamental choice to make,” Sanders said. “We can continue to allow the very rich to get much richer while everyone else gets poorer and poorer. Or we can tax the winnings a handful of billionaires made during the pandemic to improve the health and well-being of tens of millions of Americans.”
    According to the statement, after the tax, the richest 0.001% of Americans would still be left with more than $310 billion in collective wealth gains this year.
    — Amazon.com Inc.  AMZN,  -0.51% CEO Jeff Bezos,  the richest individual on the planet and whose wealth has risen 63% during the pandemic to more than $190 billion, would pay a one-time tax of $42.8 billion.
    — Tesla Inc.  TSLA,  -1.42% CEO Elon Musk, whose wealth has nearly tripled as Tesla stock has skyrocketed in recent months, would pay $27.5 million.
  — Facebook Inc.  FB,  +2.37% CEO Mark Zuckerberg, whose wealth has risen by nearly $40 billion during the pandemic, would pay $22.8 billion.
  — The Walton family, heirs to the Walmart Inc.  WMT,  +0.04% fortune whose wealth has grown by $21 billion, would pay $12.9 billion.
  “Incredibly,” the senators said in a statement, “these billionaires currently pay a lower effective tax rate than teachers or truck drivers.”
  No one with a net worth of less than $1 billion would pay the tax, they said.
  “Requiring billionaires to pay their fair share will help support workers and families dealing with job losses, food insecurity, housing instability and health care,” Gillibrand said. “Not only is this a common-sense proposal, but it’s a moral one and Congress should be doing all we can to assist Americans struggling right now.”
  Taxing the ultra-rich has become a popular cry for Democrats following the Trump tax cuts of 2017. Last year, Sen. Elizabeth Warren, D.-Mass., proposed  a tax on “ultra millionaires” as part of her presidential campaign.
  It should be noted that,  as MarketWatch reported earlier this year, starting the clock at March 18 lacks some context, as investors started worrying about the pandemic in February, leading to the stock market’s temporary but steep tumble.
  For the year, the Dow Jones Industrial Average  DJIA,  -0.31% is down 4%, while the S&P 500  SPX,  -0.07% is up nearly 4% and the Nasdaq Composite  COMP,  -0.21% has gained 24%.
